summary:
The Avalanche is officially open for business 🌍 and Game Day 1 delivered exactly what you'd expect from an opening fixture — one goal, not much else, and a result that's already making some of you very nervous.

Mexico did what Tier 1 teams are supposed to do and ground out three points against a South Africa side that tried hard and went home with nothing. Whoever drew Mexico is sitting quietly smug right now 😏 — and rightly so. Whoever drew South Africa has already started eyeing up the Beautiful Loser prize as a consolation plan.

On the Wooden Spoon watch, South Africa have helpfully opened their goals conceded column on Day 1. Someone out there is already on the board ⚠️ — you know who you are.

Game Day 2 tomorrow. The Avalanche builds. 🏔️
